rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Class Notice;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES Motion For Approval Of Class Notice Plaintiff's Motion for Approval of Class Notice is denied with 30 days leave to amend. While the burdens of discovery are rarely imposed upon absent Plaintiff class members, (Phillips Petroleum Co. v. Shutts (1985) 472 U.S. 797, 810), CRC Rule 3.768 explicitly permits such discovery. Thus, although unlikely, discovery is possible and the language indicating that no action need be taken as a participant should be excluded. However, Defendant's proposed addition of language indicating that such discovery may be required is similarly not useful to the purpose of this notice and need not be added. In addition, in balancing the interests of adequate time for trial preparation with the importance of providing adequate time for class members to review and consider whether to opt out of the class, the notice period is set at 60 days. Moreover, requiring opt out requests to be mailed is itself not unduly burdensome, but here the notice seems to require specific language in that mailing. Instead, a less burdensome opt-out provision should be used, stating that: "The request should state that you want to be excluded from Mathies v. Union Bank." The notice should be corrected with respect to the courthouse hours to reflect the correct hours (8:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.). Furthermore, while neither the pre-approval of an outer envelope nor a plan for re-mailing returned notices is required, both increase the chance of notifying Plaintiff-class members. However, requiring these steps at this stage of the proceedings would be unfairly prejudicial to Plaintiffs given Defendant's failure to raise either issue in the six months since it received the notice for review, and are therefore not required. Finally, Defendant's request for an order prohibiting class counsel from communicating with class members during the notice period is denied. Defendant points to no authority prohibiting such communications by class counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: ROWENA SETO V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses are not factually devoid. Even if the Court assumes that the burden shifted, the declarations of Mr. Morgan and Dr. Kelkar create a triable issue of material fact regarding defendant's actual and/or constructive notice. The cryptic work orders do not clearly show that defendant repaired the portion of the track where plaintiff was injured. The design immunity defense does not apply because plaintiff is complaining about deteriorated concrete, not the design of the bike lane. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: VIC-1 CONSTRUCTION, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Response To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Req. Thtat Order Be Confirmed
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 3, RESPONDENT NARENDRA (TONY) SINGH Ntc Of Hearing On Response To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Req. Thtat Order Be Confirmed Granted. No opposition filed. The Arbitration Award is confirmed. The Petition to Vacate was served more than 100 days after the Award was served on Respondent and is therefore untimely. (CCP sec. 1288.) Additionally, Petitioner never scheduled a hearing date or served notice of a hearing on Respondent. (CCP sec. 1290.4.) rulings.Case Title: BRAD D SANDERS VS. EDWARD ZUSMAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ATTORNEY LICENSED TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Markun Zusman Compton Llp To Comply With Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BRAD SANDERS' Motion For Order Compelling Markun Zusman Compton LLP To Comply With Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied, except as to production of legible documents. The Court finds that all responsive documents within the descriptive categories of Plaintiff's Demand for Production nos. 4, 5, and 6, created after September 22, 2011, would be protected from discovery as work product. Travelers Ins. Co. V. Superior Court (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 436, 452. The Court finds that under the facts of this case, where the defendants state they produced all responsive documents before that date, no privilege log is required. To the extent any documents previously produced are illegible due to poor copy or scan quality, defendants shall produce legible copies (or images) within 14 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL et al VS. IRELAND INTERIOR SYSTEMS, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANTS MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA VAN BEMMEL, RICHARD WOODS, CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORAT AND SERENA VAN BEMMEL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained, without leave to amend, as to the first cause of action as to Michael Ireland (CCP § 430.10(b)), and overruled as to all remaining grounds and causes of action. The claims are not barred by the statute of limitations; Cross-Complainants allege unauthorized transfers occurring until February 2013. (First Am. Cross-Complaint, 22.) For the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, Michael Ireland voluntarily withdraws his cause of action as an individual only. (Opp., p. 3:22-23.) For the second - sixth, eighth causes of action, although unjust enrichment is not a cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege restitution. (Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1370.) For the seventh cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege conversion based on a specific, identifiable sum of money. (PCO, Inc. v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacobs, Glaser, Weil & Shapiro, LLP (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 384, 395.) For the ninth cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege an actual, present controversy based on the two agreements. On the one hand, in the Common Stock Subscription Agreement (First Am. Cross-Complaint, Exhibit A), Michelle Van Bemmel's shares of stock were to be held in a voting trust until her loan was paid off. On the other hand, in the Voting Trust Agreement (id., at Exhibit B), the Agreement was to expire on September 1, 2012. Thus, an actual, present controversy exists between the parties as to their rights and duties under the two agreements. (See First Am. Complaint, Exhibits A, B.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO et al VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 9, 2014, Line 19, PLAINTIFF CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO, MAMIE HOW, RITA JONES, LEE SMITH, GARY TOM Req For Prelim Injunction The motion for preliminary injunction is granted. Plaintiffs are likely to prevail on their declaratory relief cause of action alleging that respondent's deprivation of the retirement benefits without a hearing violated plaintiffs' due process rights. See Petrillo v. BART (1988) 197 Cal.App.3d 798, 808; Goldberg v. Kelly (1970) 397 U.S. 254; Skelly v. State Personnel Bd. (1975) 15 Cal.3d 194. The balance of equities and harms weighs in plaintiffs' favor. The plaintiffs rely on the benefits to support themselves. If respondent ultimately prevails, then it can easily recoup the overpayments. The motion for writ of mandate is denied as moot.
